Functions for initializing and accessing various application library states in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file provides function declarations and type definitions for managing various application services within a kernel or application library. It includes functions to initialize the application library ([`kernel_applib_init`](<#kernel_applib_init>)), and to access or manage states related to animation, logging, compass configuration, event services, tick timer services, connection services, and battery state services. The file also defines a type `Layer` and provides a function to retrieve a stack of layer trees ([`kernel_applib_get_layer_tree_stack`](<#kernel_applib_get_layer_tree_stack>)). The header includes several private and public service headers, indicating that it interfaces with both internal and external components of the application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/animation_private.h`
- `kernel/logging_private.h`
- `applib/accel_service_private.h`
- `applib/tick_timer_service_private.h`
- `applib/compass_service_private.h`
- `applib/battery_state_service.h`
- `applib/battery_state_service_private.h`
- `applib/connection_service.h`
- `applib/connection_service_private.h`

### kernel\_applib\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:kernel_applib_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/kernel_applib_state.h#L29>)

Initializes application library components.
- **Description**: Use this function to initialize the necessary components of the application library before any other operations. It sets up internal states and services required for the application to function correctly. Call this function once at the start of the application to ensure all services are ready for use. This function must be called before using any other functions that depend on the initialized services.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`kernel_applib_init`](<kernel_applib_state.c.md#kernel_applib_init>)  (Implementation)

### kernel\_applib\_get\_log\_state<!-- {{#callable_declaration:kernel_applib_get_log_state}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/kernel_applib_state.h#L33>)

Retrieves the current logging state for the kernel application library.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the current logging state, which is necessary for performing logging operations within the kernel application library. It must be called in a context where logging is safe, such as outside of interrupt service routines or critical sections, and after the FreeRTOS scheduler has started. The function handles different phases of operation, ensuring that logging is only performed when it is safe to do so. If the function is called in an unsafe context or if logging is already in progress, it returns `NULL`. Ensure that `kernel_applib_init` has been called before using this function to ensure proper initialization.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a `LogState` structure if successful, or `NULL` if logging is not possible or already in progress.
- **See Also**: [`kernel_applib_get_log_state`](<kernel_applib_state.c.md#kernel_applib_get_log_state>)  (Implementation)


---
### kernel\_applib\_release\_log\_state<!-- {{#callable_declaration:kernel_applib_release_log_state}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/kernel_applib_state.h#L35>)

Releases the log state and clears task-specific flags.
- **Description**: Use this function to release a previously acquired log state and reset any task-specific re-entrancy flags. This function must be called when the log state is no longer needed to ensure proper resource management. It is important to call this function outside of critical sections and not from an interrupt service routine (ISR) unless the system is in phase 1 or no mutex is available. This function also handles the unlocking of a mutex if applicable.
- **Inputs**:
    - `state`: A pointer to a `LogState` structure that represents the log state to release. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the `LogState` object.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`kernel_applib_release_log_state`](<kernel_applib_state.c.md#kernel_applib_release_log_state>)  (Implementation)

### kernel\_applib\_get\_compass\_config<!-- {{#callable_declaration:kernel_applib_get_compass_config}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/kernel_applib_state.h#L37>)

Provides access to the compass service configuration.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain a pointer to the compass service configuration structure. This function is useful when you need to access or modify the compass service settings. Ensure that the compass service is initialized before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a `CompassServiceConfig` structure, which can be used to access or modify the compass service configuration.
- **See Also**: [`kernel_applib_get_compass_config`](<kernel_applib_state.c.md#kernel_applib_get_compass_config>)  (Implementation)

### kernel\_applib\_get\_event\_service\_state<!-- {{#callable_declaration:kernel_applib_get_event_service_state}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/kernel/kernel_applib_state.h#L39>)

Provides access to the current event service state.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain a pointer to the current state of the event service. This function returns a pointer to a static `EventServiceInfo` structure, which means the data is shared across all calls to this function. It is important to note that the caller must not attempt to free or modify the returned pointer, as it is managed internally. This function is useful when you need to inspect or utilize the current state of the event service within the application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a static `EventServiceInfo` structure representing the current state of the event service.
- **See Also**: [`kernel_applib_get_event_service_state`](<kernel_applib_state.c.md#kernel_applib_get_event_service_state>)  (Implementation)

Provides access to the tick timer service state.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain a pointer to the `TickTimerServiceState` structure, which represents the current state of the tick timer service. This function returns a pointer to a static instance, so the caller must not attempt to free or modify the returned pointer. It is safe to call this function multiple times, and it will always return the same instance.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to a `TickTimerServiceState` structure representing the tick timer service state.
- **See Also**: [`kernel_applib_get_tick_timer_service_state`](<kernel_applib_state.c.md#kernel_applib_get_tick_timer_service_state>)  (Implementation)
